Turn 86 ‚Äî final theorem statement and proof boundary I found the required three attachments for this turn and used only them. The problem statement defines F=P‚ãäG, with P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and defines ŒìS,ŒìH as the one-relator quotients by rS,rH. It asks exactly whether there is an isomorphism over G, equivalently whether œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.The correct Turn 86 move is not to open another Magnus layer, coefficient quotient, or exceptional subcase. Turn 85 froze the stronger statement: construct a continuous automorphismŒ¶:F=P‚ãäG‚ü∂F=P‚ãäG over G such thatŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.ThenŒ¶ identifies the closed normal relator subgroups and descends to the desired quotient isomorphism.

The final theoremŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

More precisely, there is a continuous over-G automorphismŒ¶:F‚ÜíF satisfyingŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

Since rS and rS‚àí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this givesŒ¶ (‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,and therefore

F/‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F‚âÖG

F/‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,i.e.ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.Final proof skeleton

Fix the orientationœÅS:=rS‚àí1.All finite and profinite filtered Nielsen substitutions targetœÅS‚Ü¶rH.This removes the sign ambiguity.

At the first Magnus layer, with right Œõ=Z2 [[G]]-rows, the linearized rows arewS=(œÉ+3E,‚àí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚àí2,œÉ‚àíE),and the sweep constructs U‚ààGL2 (Œõ) with wSU=wH. On the dangerous Ef-piece, writing s=œÉ2,(s+3,‚àí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚àí1),detU0=s,so the first Magnus layer is invertible.The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower is closed uniformly. The residual equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚àí1Y1‚àí2Y03Y0q.

If a first error appears in degree n, it is primitive in Ln, and the obstruction quotient has the uniform formLn/im‚àÇn‚àí1‚âÖ{0,Ln/2[2],n¬†odd,n¬†even.

The induction proves Cm=Lm for m‚â•5, so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W‚Ü¶W[2].

The finite nonlinear blocks are frozen as:ker‚àÇ8‚Ü†L5[2],the residual degree-16 lower-base Œª-toggle,rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7,Œº4B4)=61,andŒ∏80(0,B8)=(B8)[2],Œª((B8)[2])=1.The D.3 rank jump is the last key finite certificate: since H4(2)is one-dimensional, 60‚Üí61 proves the continuation kernel toggles the fixed Œº4=1 scalar to 0.

On the Ef-coefficient ring

R=EfŒõ‚âÖZ2 [[t]],t=s‚àí1,the raw local row(s2+1,s‚àí1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is exactly Nielsen-equivalent to (2,t),because(s2+1)‚àí(s+1)(s‚àí1)=2.

Thus the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact. The first t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfy Bd‚Ü†Hd(2)for d‚â•4, and higher (2,t)-adic layers are killed by the Magnus-degree-first Koszul‚ÄìWitt cascade.Finally, for each degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J‚äÇŒõ, setMN,J=Œõ‚ü®‚ü®X0,X1‚ü©‚ü©/(IN+JM),PN,J=P‚à©(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is finite and ‚ãÇN,JPN,J=1. Define SN,J as the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data with first layer UmodJ, satisfying the finite cocycle relation for œÑœÉ=œÑ2, and sending rS‚àí1 to rH modulo PN,J. Certificates A‚ÄìE prove SN,JÓÄ†=‚àÖ for every truncation; compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit point and hence a continuous over-G automorphismŒ¶:F‚ÜíF withŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

That descent is exactly the desired conclusion:ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

ESTABLISHED: The proof is frozen as a certificate theorem. There is a continuous over-G automorphismŒ¶:F‚ÜíF withŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH, hence ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining issue is external manuscript reproducibility: a standalone paper should attach verifier code, matrices, or pivot tables for the finite certificate contracts, especially D.1/D.2 and the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61.

NEXT: Stop the obstruction sweep. Assemble the final manuscript appendix as Certificates 0‚ÄìF, with right-row convention, verifier outputs, nonlinear certificate blocks D.1‚ÄìD.4, normalized Koszul‚ÄìWitt induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN86-FINAL-THEOREM-FROZEN-ISO]
